v.CNV: 雅各举目观看，见以扫带着四百人来了；他就把孩子分开三队，交给利亚、拉结和两个婢女。
v.ESV: And Jacob lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, Esau was coming, and four hundred men with him. So he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two female servants.
v.GAE: 야곱이 눈을 들어 보니 에서가 사백 명의 장정을 거느리고 오고 있는지라 그의 자식들을 나누어 레아와 라헬과 두 여종에게 맡기고
v.KJV: And Jacob lifted up his eyes, and looked, and, behold, Esau came, and with him four hundred men. And he divided the children unto Leah, and unto Rachel, and unto the two handmaids.
v.NASB: Then Jacob lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, Esau was coming, and four hundred men with him. So he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two maids.
v.NIV: Jacob looked up and there was Esau, coming with his four hundred men; so he divided the children among Leah, Rachel and the two female servants.
v.NRSV: Now Jacob looked up and saw Esau coming, and four hundred men with him. So he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two maids.
v.SAENEW: 야곱이 고개를 들어 보니, 에서가 장정 사백 명을 거느리고 오고 있었다. 야곱은, 아이들을 레아와 라헬과 두 여종에게 나누어서 맡기고,
v.WLB: 야곱이 눈을 들어 보니 에서가 400명을 거느리고 다가오고 있었습니다. 야곱은 자식들을 레아와 라헬과 두 여종에게 나눠 맡겼습니다.

v.CNV: 他叫两个婢女和她们的孩子走在前头，利亚和她的孩子跟着，拉结和约瑟走在最后。
v.ESV: And he put the servants with their children in front, then Leah with her children, and Rachel and Joseph last of all.
v.GAE: 여종들과 그들의 자식들은 앞에 두고 레아와 그의 자식들은 다음에 두고 라헬과 요셉은 뒤에 두고
v.KJV: And he put the handmaids and their children foremost, and Leah and her children after, and Rachel and Joseph hindermost.
v.NASB: He put the maids and their children in front, and Leah and her children next, and Rachel and Joseph last.
v.NIV: He put the female servants and their children in front, Leah and her children next, and Rachel and Joseph in the rear.
v.NRSV: He put the maids with their children in front, then Leah with her children, and Rachel and Joseph last of all.
v.SAENEW: 두 여종과 그들에게서 난 아이들은 앞에 세우고, 레아와 그에게서 난 아이들은 그 뒤에 세우고, 라헬과 요셉은 맨 뒤에 세워서 따라오게 하였다.
v.WLB: 그는 두 여종과 그 자식들을 맨 앞에 세우고 레아와 그 자식들을 그 다음에 세웠으며 라헬과 요셉은 맨 뒤에 세웠습니다.

v.CNV: 他自己却走在他们前面，七次俯伏在地，直到走近他哥哥跟前。
v.ESV: He himself went on before them, bowing himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.
v.GAE: 자기는 그들 앞에서 나아가되 몸을 일곱 번 땅에 굽히며 그의 형 에서에게 가까이 가니
v.KJV: And he passed over before them, and bowed himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.
v.NASB: But he himself passed on ahead of them and bowed down to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.
v.NIV: He himself went on ahead and bowed down to the ground seven times as he approached his brother.
v.NRSV: He himself went on ahead of them, bowing himself to the ground seven times, until he came near his brother.
v.SAENEW: 야곱은 맨 앞으로 나가서 형에게로 가까이 가면서, 일곱 번이나 땅에 엎드려 절을 하였다.
v.WLB: 야곱은 그들의 맨 앞으로 나아갔습니다. 그는 땅에 엎드리어 일곱 번 절하며 자기 형에게 다가갔습니다.

v.CNV: 以扫跑来迎接他，拥抱他，伏在他颈项上，与他亲嘴；两个人都哭了。
v.ESV: But Esau ran to meet him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.
v.GAE: 에서가 달려와서 그를 맞이하여 안고 목을 어긋맞추어 그와 입맞추고 서로 우니라
v.KJV: And Esau ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fell on his neck, and kissed him: and they wept.
v.NASB: Then Esau ran to meet him and embraced him, and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.
v.NIV: But Esau ran to meet Jacob and embraced him; he threw his arms around his neck and kissed him. And they wept.
v.NRSV: But Esau ran to meet him, and embraced him, and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 에서가 달려와서, 그를 끌어안았다. 에서는 두 팔을 벌려, 야곱의 목을 끌어안고서, 입을 맞추고, 둘은 함께 울었다.
v.WLB: 에서가 달려와 야곱을 맞았습니다. 그는 야곱을 부둥켜안으며 두 팔로 야곱의 목을 끌어안고 입 맞추었습니다. 그들은 함께 울었습니다.

v.CNV: 以扫举目观看，看见妇人和孩子，就问：“这些与你同来的人是谁？”雅各回答：“是　神赐给你仆人的孩子。”
v.ESV: And when Esau lifted up his eyes and saw the women and children, he said, “Who are these with you?” Jacob said, “The children whom God has graciously given your servant.”
v.GAE: 에서가 눈을 들어 여인들과 자식들을 보고 묻되 너와 함께 한 이들은 누구냐 야곱이 이르되 하나님이 주의 종에게 은혜로 주신 자식들이니이다
v.KJV: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw the women and the children; and said, Who are those with thee? And he said, The children which God hath graciously given thy servant.
v.NASB: He lifted his eyes and saw the women and the children, and said, 'Who are these with you?' So he said, 'The children whom God has graciously given your servant.'
v.NIV: Then Esau looked up and saw the women and children. “Who are these with you?” he asked. Jacob answered, “They are the children God has graciously given your servant. ”
v.NRSV: When Esau looked up and saw the women and children, he said, “Who are these with you?” Jacob said, “The children whom God has graciously given your servant.”
v.SAENEW: 에서가 고개를 들어, 여인들과 아이들을 보면서 물었다. "네가 데리고 온 이 사람들은 누구냐?" 야곱이 대답하였다. "이것들은 하나님이 형님의 못난 아우에게 은혜로 주신 자식들입니다."
v.WLB: 에서가 눈을 들어 여자들과 아이들을 보고 물었습니다. “이 사람들은 누구냐?” 야곱이 대답했습니다. “하나님께서 형님의 종에게 은혜를 베푸셔서 주신 자식들입니다.”

v.CNV: 于是，两个婢女和她们的孩子上前下拜。
v.ESV: Then the servants drew near, they and their children, and bowed down.
v.GAE: 그 때에 여종들이 그의 자식들과 더불어 나아와 절하고
v.KJV: Then the handmaidens came near, they and their children, and they bowed themselves.
v.NASB: Then the maids came near with their children, and they bowed down.
v.NIV: Then the female servants and their children approached and bowed down.
v.NRSV: Then the maids drew near, they and their children, and bowed down;
v.SAENEW: 그러자 두 여종과 그들에게서 난 아이들이 앞으로 나와서, 엎드려 절을 하였다.
v.WLB: 그때 여종들과 그들의 아이들이 나와서 절 했습니다.

v.CNV: 利亚和她的孩子也上前下拜，随后，约瑟和拉结也上前下拜。
v.ESV: Leah likewise and her children drew near and bowed down. And last Joseph and Rachel drew near, and they bowed down.
v.GAE: 레아도 그의 자식들과 더불어 나아와 절하고 그 후에 요셉이 라헬과 더불어 나아와 절하니
v.KJV: And Leah also with her children came near, and bowed themselves: and after came Joseph near and Rachel, and they bowed themselves.
v.NASB: Leah likewise came near with her children, and they bowed down; and afterward Joseph came near with Rachel, and they bowed down.
v.NIV: Next, Leah and her children came and bowed down. Last of all came Joseph and Rachel, and they too bowed down.
v.NRSV: Leah likewise and her children drew near and bowed down; and finally Joseph and Rachel drew near, and they bowed down.
v.SAENEW: 다음에는 레아와 그에게서 난 아이들이 앞으로 나와서, 엎드려 절을 하였다. 마지막으로 요셉과 라헬이 나와서, 그들도 엎드려 절을 하였다.
v.WLB: 다음으로 레아와 그녀의 아이들이 나와서 절했습니다. 마지막으로 요셉과 라헬이 나와서 절을 했습니다.

v.CNV: 以扫又问：“我遇见的这一群牲畜，是什么意思呢？”雅各回答：“是要讨我主喜悦的。”
v.ESV: Esau said, “What do you mean by all this company that I met?” Jacob answered, “To find favor in the sight of my lord.”
v.GAE: 에서가 또 이르되 내가 만난 바 이 모든 떼는 무슨 까닭이냐 야곱이 이르되 내 주께 은혜를 입으려 함이니이다
v.KJV: And he said, What meanest thou by all this drove which I met? And he said, These are to find grace in the sight of my lord.
v.NASB: And he said, 'What do you mean by all this company which I have met?' And he said, 'To find favor in the sight of my lord.'
v.NIV: Esau asked, “What’s the meaning of all these flocks and herds I met?” “To find favor in your eyes, my lord,” he said.
v.NRSV: Esau said, “What do you mean by all this company that I met?” Jacob answered, “To find favor with my lord.”
v.SAENEW: 에서가 물었다. "내가 오는 길에 만난 가축 떼는 모두 웬 것이냐?" 야곱이 대답하였다. "형님께 은혜를 입고 싶어서, 가지고 온 것입니다."
v.WLB: 에서가 물었습니다. “내가 만난 이 가축 무리들은 다 무엇이냐?” 야곱이 대답했습니다. “제 주인이신 형님께 은총을 입고자 가져온 것입니다.”

v.CNV: 以扫说：“弟弟啊，我已经很富有了，你的仍归你吧。”
v.ESV: But Esau said, “I have enough, my brother; keep what you have for yourself.”
v.GAE: 에서가 이르되 내 동생아 내게 있는 것이 족하니 네 소유는 네게 두라
v.KJV: And Esau said, I have enough, my brother; keep that thou hast unto thyself.
v.NASB: But Esau said, 'I have plenty, my brother; let what you have be your own.'
v.NIV: But Esau said, “I already have plenty, my brother. Keep what you have for yourself.”
v.NRSV: But Esau said, “I have enough, my brother; keep what you have for yourself.”
v.SAENEW: 에서가 말하였다. "아우야, 나는 넉넉하다. 너의 것은 네가 가져라."
v.WLB: 그러자 에서가 말했습니다. “내 동생아, 나는 이미 가진 것이 많다. 네 것이니 네가 가져라.”

v.CNV: 雅各说：“请不要这样，我若得你的喜悦，就求你从我手里收下这些礼物吧；因为我看见了你的面，就好像见了　神的面；你实在恩待了我。
v.ESV: Jacob said, “No, please, if I have found favor in your sight, then accept my present from my hand. For I have seen your face, which is like seeing the face of God, and you have accepted me.
v.GAE: 야곱이 이르되 그렇지 아니하니이다 내가 형님의 눈앞에서 은혜를 입었사오면 청하건대 내 손에서 이 예물을 받으소서 내가 형님의 얼굴을 뵈온즉 하나님의 얼굴을 본 것 같사오며 형님도 나를 기뻐하심이니이다
v.KJV: And Jacob said, Nay, I pray thee, if now I have found grace in thy sight, then receive my present at my hand: for therefore I have seen thy face, as though I had seen the face of God, and thou wast pleased with me.
v.NASB: Jacob said, 'No, please, if now I have found favor in your sight, then take my present from my hand, for I see your face as one sees the face of God, and you have received me favorably.
v.NIV: “No, please!” said Jacob. “If I have found favor in your eyes, accept this gift from me. For to see your face is like seeing the face of God, now that you have received me favorably.
v.NRSV: Jacob said, “No, please; if I find favor with you, then accept my present from my hand; for truly to see your face is like seeing the face of God — since you have received me with such favor.
v.SAENEW: 야곱이 말하였다. "아닙니다, 형님, 형님께서 저를 좋게 보시면, 제가 드리는 이 선물을 받아 주십시오. 형님께서 저를 이렇게 너그럽게 맞아 주시니, 형님의 얼굴을 뵙는 것이 하나님의 얼굴을 뵙는 듯합니다.
v.WLB: 야곱이 말했습니다. “아닙니다. 제가 형님의 은총을 입었다면 제가 드리는 이 선물을 받아 주십시오. 저를 반갑게 맞아 주시는 형님을 보니 마치 하나님의 얼굴을 보는 것 같습니다.

v.CNV: 请你收下我带来给你的礼物，因为　神施恩给我，我什么都有了。”雅各再三恳求他，他才收下了。
v.ESV: Please accept my blessing that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough.” Thus he urged him, and he took it.
v.GAE: 하나님이 내게 은혜를 베푸셨고 내 소유도 족하오니 청하건대 내가 형님께 드리는 예물을 받으소서 하고 그에게 강권하매 받으니라
v.KJV: Take, I pray thee, my blessing that is brought to thee; because God hath dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough. And he urged him, and he took it.
v.NASB: 'Please take my gift which has been brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me and because I have plenty.' Thus he urged him and he took it.
v.NIV: Please accept the present that was brought to you, for God has been gracious to me and I have all I need.” And because Jacob insisted, Esau accepted it.
v.NRSV: Please accept my gift that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have everything I want.” So he urged him, and he took it.
v.SAENEW: 하나님이 저에게 은혜를 베푸시므로, 제가 가진 것도 이렇게 넉넉하게 되었습니다. 그러니 제가 형님께 가지고 온 이 선물을 기꺼이 받아 주시기 바랍니다." 야곱이 간곡히 권하므로, 에서는 그 선물을 받았다.
v.WLB: 제발 형님께 드리는 이 선물을 받아 주십시오. 하나님께서 제게 은혜를 베푸셔서 저도 넉넉합니다.” 야곱이 끝까지 권하자 에서가 받았습니다.

v.CNV: 以扫说：“我们起程前行吧，我和你一起走。”
v.ESV: Then Esau said, “Let us journey on our way, and I will go ahead of you.”
v.GAE: 에서가 이르되 우리가 떠나자 내가 너와 동행하리라
v.KJV: And he said, Let us take our journey, and let us go, and I will go before thee.
v.NASB: Then Esau said, 'Let us take our journey and go, and I will go before you.'
v.NIV: Then Esau said, “Let us be on our way; I’ll accompany you.”
v.NRSV: Then Esau said, “Let us journey on our way, and I will go alongside you.”
v.SAENEW: 에서가 말하였다. "자, 이제 갈 길을 서두르자. 내가 앞장을 서마."
v.WLB: 에서가 말했습니다. “자, 이제 가도록 하자. 내가 앞장서마.”

v.CNV: 雅各对他说：“我主知道孩子们还年幼娇嫩；牛羊也正在乳养的时候，需要我的照顾；如果要催赶它们走一天的路，恐怕所有牲畜都要死了。
v.ESV: But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are frail, and that the nursing flocks and herds are a care to me. If they are driven hard for one day, all the flocks will die.
v.GAE: 야곱이 그에게 이르되 내 주도 아시거니와 자식들은 연약하고 내게 있는 양 떼와 소가 새끼를 데리고 있은즉 하루만 지나치게 몰면 모든 떼가 죽으리니
v.KJV: And he said unto him, My lord knoweth that the children are tender, and the flocks and herds with young are with me: and if men should overdrive them one day, all the flock will die.
v.NASB: But he said to him, 'My lord knows that the children are frail and that the flocks and herds which are nursing are a care to me. And if they are driven hard one day, all the flocks will die.
v.NIV: But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are tender and that I must care for the ewes and cows that are nursing their young. If they are driven hard just one day, all the animals will die.
v.NRSV: But Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are frail and that the flocks and herds, which are nursing, are a care to me; and if they are overdriven for one day, all the flocks will die.
v.SAENEW: 야곱이 그에게 말하였다. "형님께서도 아시다시피, 아이들이 아직 어립니다. 또 저는 새끼 딸린 양 떼와 소 떼를 돌봐야 합니다. 하루만이라도 지나치게 빨리 몰고 가면 다 죽습니다.
v.WLB: 야곱이 에서에게 말했습니다. “형님께서도 아시지 않습니까? 제 아이들은 약합니다. 그리고 제게는 젖먹이는 양과 소가 있습니다. 하루라도 더 무리해 몰고 가면 모든 가축이 다 죽을 것입니다.

v.CNV: 请我主在仆人前头先行；我要照着在我面前群畜和孩子们的速度，慢慢前行，直走到西珥我主那里去。”
v.ESV: Let my lord pass on ahead of his servant, and I will lead on slowly, at the pace of the livestock that are ahead of me and at the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir.”
v.GAE: 청하건대 내 주는 종보다 앞서 가소서 나는 앞에 가는 가축과 자식들의 걸음대로 천천히 인도하여 세일로 가서 내 주께 나아가리이다
v.KJV: Let my lord, I pray thee, pass over before his servant: and I will lead on softly, according as the cattle that goeth before me and the children be able to endure, until I come unto my lord unto Seir.
v.NASB: 'Please let my lord pass on before his servant, and I will proceed at my leisure, according to the pace of the cattle that are before me and according to the pace of the children, until I come to my lord at Seir.'
v.NIV: So let my lord go on ahead of his servant, while I move along slowly at the pace of the flocks and herds before me and the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir. ”
v.NRSV: Let my lord pass on ahead of his servant, and I will lead on slowly, according to the pace of the cattle that are before me and according to the pace of the children, until I come to my lord in Seir.”
v.SAENEW: 형님께서는 이 아우보다 앞서서 떠나십시오. 그렇게 하시면, 저는 앞에 가는 이 가축 떼와 아이들을 이끌고, 그들의 걸음에 맞추어 천천히 세일로 가서, 형님께 나가겠습니다."
v.WLB: 그러니 내 주께서 이 종보다 먼저 가십시오. 저는 제 앞에 있는 가축들과 아이들이 가는 속도에 맞춰 천천히 가다가 세일에서 내 주를 만나겠습니다.”

v.CNV: 以扫说：“让我把跟随我的人留几个在你这里吧。”雅各说：“为什么要这样呢？只要我得我主的喜悦就是了。”
v.ESV: So Esau said, “Let me leave with you some of the people who are with me.” But he said, “What need is there? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord.”
v.GAE: 에서가 이르되 내가 내 종 몇 사람을 네게 머물게 하리라 야곱이 이르되 어찌하여 그리하리이까 나로 내 주께 은혜를 얻게 하소서 하매
v.KJV: And Esau said, Let me now leave with thee some of the folk that are with me. And he said, What needeth it? let me find grace in the sight of my lord.
v.NASB: Esau said, 'Please let me leave with you some of the people who are with me.' But he said, 'What need is there? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord.'
v.NIV: Esau said, “Then let me leave some of my men with you.” “But why do that?” Jacob asked. “Just let me find favor in the eyes of my lord.”
v.NRSV: So Esau said, “Let me leave with you some of the people who are with me.” But he said, “Why should my lord be so kind to me?”
v.SAENEW: 에서가 말하였다. "그렇다면, 내가 나의 부하 몇을 너와 같이 가게 하겠다." 야곱이 말렸다. "그러실 것까지는 없습니다. 형님께서 저를 너그럽게 맞아 주신 것만으로도 만족합니다."
v.WLB: 에서가 말했습니다. “그렇다면 내 사람들을 네게 좀 남겨 놓고 가겠다.” 야곱이 말했습니다. “그렇게까지 하지 않으셔도 됩니다. 그저 내 주께서 저를 너그럽게 받아 주신 것만으로도 저는 좋습니다.”

v.CNV: 于是，以扫在那一天回西珥去了。
v.ESV: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.
v.GAE: 이 날에 에서는 세일로 돌아가고
v.KJV: So Esau returned that day on his way unto Seir.
v.NASB: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.
v.NIV: So that day Esau started on his way back to Seir.
v.NRSV: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.
v.SAENEW: 그 날로 에서는 길을 떠나 세일로 돌아갔고,
v.WLB: 그날 에서는 길을 떠나 세일로 돌아갔습니다.

v.CNV: 雅各却起程到疏割去，在那里为自己建造了一座房子，又为牲畜搭了些草棚，因此给那地方起名叫疏割。
v.ESV: But Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built himself a house and made booths for his livestock. Therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
v.GAE: 야곱은 숙곳에 이르러 자기를 위하여 집을 짓고 그의 가축을 위하여 우릿간을 지었으므로 그 땅 이름을 숙곳이라 부르더라
v.KJV: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built him an house, and made booths for his cattle: therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
v.NASB: Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built for himself a house and made booths for his livestock; therefore the place is named Succoth.
v.NIV: Jacob, however, went to Sukkoth, where he built a place for himself and made shelters for his livestock. That is why the place is called Sukkoth.
v.NRSV: But Jacob journeyed to Succoth, and built himself a house, and made booths for his cattle; therefore the place is called Succoth.
v.SAENEW: 야곱은 숙곳으로 갔다. 거기에서 야곱은 자기들이 살 집과 짐승이 바람을 피할 우리를 지었다. 그래서 그 곳 이름이 숙곳이 되었다.
v.WLB: 그러나 야곱은 숙곳으로 갔습니다. 야곱은 그곳에 집을 짓고 가축 우리도 만들었습니다. 그래서 사람들이 그곳의 이름을 숙곳이라고 불렀습니다.

v.CNV: 雅各从巴旦．亚兰回来的时候，平平安安地到了迦南地的示剑城，在城的对面支搭了帐棚。
v.ESV: And Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, on his way from Paddan-aram, and he camped before the city.
v.GAE: 야곱이 밧단아람에서부터 평안히 가나안 땅 세겜 성읍에 이르러 그 성읍 앞에 장막을 치고
v.KJV: And Jacob came to Shalem, a city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Padan-aram; and pitched his tent before the city.
v.NASB: Now Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Paddan-aram, and camped before the city.
v.NIV: After Jacob came from Paddan Aram, he arrived safely at the city of Shechem in Canaan and camped within sight of the city.
v.NRSV: Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, on his way from Paddan-aram; and he camped before the city.
v.SAENEW: 야곱이 밧단아람을 떠나, 가나안 땅의 세겜 성에 무사히 이르러서, 그 성 앞에다가 장막을 쳤다.
v.WLB: 이처럼 야곱이 밧단아람에서 돌아와서 가나안 땅 세겜 성에 무사히 도착해 그 성 앞에 장막을 쳤습니다.

v.CNV: 他支搭帐棚的那块地，是他用一百块银子向示剑的父亲哈抹的子孙买来的。
v.ESV: And from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, he bought for a hundred pieces of money the piece of land on which he had pitched his tent.
v.GAE: 그가 장막을 친 밭을 세겜의 아버지 하몰의 아들들의 손에서 백 크시타에 샀으며
v.KJV: And he bought a parcel of a field, where he had spread his tent, at the hand of the children of Hamor, Shechem’s father, for an hundred pieces of money.
v.NASB: He bought the piece of land where he had pitched his tent from the hand of the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, for one hundred pieces of money.
v.NIV: For a hundred pieces of silver, he bought from the sons of Hamor, the father of Shechem, the plot of ground where he pitched his tent.
v.NRSV: And from the sons of Hamor, Shechem’s father, he bought for one hundred pieces of money the plot of land on which he had pitched his tent.
v.SAENEW: 야곱은, 장막을 친 그 밭을, 세겜의 아버지인 하몰의 아들들에게서 은 백 냥을 주고 샀다.
v.WLB: 그는 자기가 장막을 친 그 들판의 일부를 세겜의 아버지 하몰의 아들들에게 은 100개를 주고 샀습니다.

v.CNV: 在那里雅各建了一座坛，给它起名叫伊利．伊罗伊．以色列。
v.ESV: There he erected an altar and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
v.GAE: 거기에 제단을 쌓고 그 이름을 엘엘로헤이스라엘이라 불렀더라
v.KJV: And he erected there an altar, and called it El-elohe-Israel.
v.NASB: Then he erected there an altar and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
v.NIV: There he set up an altar and called it El Elohe Israel.
v.NRSV: There he erected an altar and called it El-Elohe-Israel.
v.SAENEW: 야곱은 거기에서 제단을 쌓고, 그 이름을 엘엘로헤이스라엘이라고 하였다.
v.WLB: 그는 거기에 제단을 쌓고 그곳을 엘엘로헤이스라엘이라고 불렀습니다.
